steps.title: Solve the system by substitution
steps.content: From the first equation, $y=-x^2$. Substitute into $8x+16-y=0$: $$8x+16+x^2 = 0 \Rightarrow (x+4)^2 = 0.$$

steps.title: Find $x$
steps.content: So $x=-4$. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. Adding $y$ to each side of the second equation in the given system of equations yields $8x+16=y$. Substituting $8x+16$ for $y$ in the first equation yields ${x}^{2}+8x+16+10=10$. Subtracting $10$ from each side of this equation yields ${x}^{2}+8x+16=0$. This equation can be rewritten as ${\left(x+4\right)}^{2}=0$. Taking the square root of each side of this equation yields $x+4=0$. Subtracting $4$ from each side of this equation yields $x=-4$. Therefore, the value of $x$ is $-4$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $y$, not $x$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
